title: Palindrome Number (Debugging)
description:
A number may remain unchanged when its digits are reversed.
The program below attempts to verify this property, but it contains errors.

languages.python:
x = int(input())
rev = 0
while x >= 0
    rev = rev * 10 + x % 10
    x = x / 10
if rev = x:
    print("Palindrome")
else:
    print("Not Palindrome")

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock (Debugging)
description: The first line of input contains an integer N, representing the number of days.\nThe second line contains N space-separated integers representing the stock price on each day. Determine the maximum profit that can be obtained by buying on one day and selling on a later day.

languages.python:
prices = list(map(int,input().split()))
min_price = 0
profit = 0
for p in prices:
 if p < min_price:
  min_price = p
 profit = max(profit, p - min_price)
print(profit)

title: Check if a Number is Perfect Number (Debugging)
description: A perfect number is equal to the sum of its proper divisors.\nThe program tries to verify this but has errors."

languages.python:
n=int(input())

  sum=sum+i

 print("Perfect")
else

title: Bubble Sort (Debugging)
description: A list of numbers can be arranged in ascending order by repeatedly swapping adjacent elements that are in the wrong order.\nThe program below attempts to perform this sorting process but contains mistakes.

languages.python:
n=int(input())
a=list(map(int,input().split()))
for i in range(n):
 for j in range(n-i):
  if a[j]>a[j+1]
   a[j],a[j+1]=a[j+1],a[j]
print(a)

title: Check if a Number is Armstrong (Debugging)

t=n

 d=n%10
 sum=sum+d*d*d
 n=n/10

 print("Armstrong")
